title: Hyderabad Metro Expansion
tagline: 42km corridor connecting IT hub to old city

overview.description: Construct a 42km metro corridor with 28 stations, projected to serve 1.2M daily riders and reduce peak congestion by 28%.

agentSequence.label: Economic Agent
findings: ₹1,200M corridor projects 12,000 direct jobs over construction phase
Property values along corridor expected +18% by year 5
Local business revenue uplift estimated at ₹340M annually

agentSequence.label: Social Agent
findings: Underserved eastern zones gain 40-minute commute reduction
Accessibility score improves for 890,000 residents
Construction-phase displacement manageable with phased rollout

agentSequence.label: Environmental Agent
findings: Net CO₂ reduction of 42,000 tonnes/year at full ridership
Construction emissions offset within 7 years of operation
Green corridor buffers recommended at 3 station zones

agentSequence.label: Stakeholder Agent
findings: Commuters and businesses strongly support (81% / 73%)
Environmental groups conditionally support with green buffers
Government alignment high; land acquisition risks in 2 districts

agentSequence.label: Risk Agent
findings: Cost overrun probability: medium (historical infra avg +12%)
Political risk low-moderate; election cycle aligns with Phase II
Mitigation: escrow fund + independent audit committee

agentSequence.label: Future Shock Agent
findings: Metro → Reduced Traffic → Property Appreciation → Rental Inflation
Third-order effect: population migration toward corridor by 2037
Black-swan: climate event could accelerate ridership +15%

agentSequence.label: Chief Decision Officer
findings: Viability score: 74/100 — conditional approval recommended
Phased implementation reduces upfront fiscal exposure
Executive recommendation: proceed with Phase I pilot corridor

consequences.label: Traffic Reduction
consequences.description: Project Decision leads to Traffic Reduction

consequences.label: Property Value Growth
consequences.description: Traffic Reduction leads to Property Value Growth

consequences.description: Property Value Growth leads to Rental Inflation

consequences.description: Rental Inflation leads to Population Migration

headlines.subtitle: Support at 81% as migration patterns shift

milestones.narrative: Commute reduced by 40 minutes on new metro segment.

Relocated closer to city center for better mobility and career growth.
Early disruption from construction, but commute times already improving on partial routes.

recommendation.executiveSummary: Hyderabad Metro Expansion demonstrates strong economic and infrastructure returns with manageable political risk. Conditional approval recommended with phased implementation.
